content:
Session: 65 user turns, 142 assistant turns
Tools: 58 calls (Bash×40, Read×13, Edit×5)
Files touched: src/lib/map-data.ts, src/pages/map.astro, /tmp/map-desktop.png, /tmp/map-mobile.png, /tmp/map-zoom2x.png, /tmp/cluster3x.png, /tmp/m2-desktop.png, /tmp/m2-mobile.png, /tmp/m3-mobile.png, /tmp/m3-desktop.png

Final response excerpt:
Shipped (`cb1ecb6`). Now your two questions:

## 1. The zoom — done
Desktop now opens one notch tighter (fills the frame), and it's self-correcting: on mobile, where +1 would crop the campus spread, it falls back to showing every pin. Verified both above.

## 2. How the whole thing looks on mobile

Honest read from the screenshot — the **map itself is fine** (all pins, readable streets, WiFi pin less jarring at this size). But the **page around it** has one real issue:

- **The hero eats the whole first screen.** "The campus is your backyard" + the paragraph fill the entire viewport, so the map starts almost entirely below the fold — you scroll past a wall of text before you see a single pin. On a page whose *whole point* is the map, the map should be the thing you land on.
- **Map is sho…
